title: Optimized ERC1155 safeBatchTransferFrom with inline YUL balance loop and calldatacopy event emission
explanation: Key optimizations over naive Solidity: (1) Slot seeds — `or(_ERC1155_MASTER_SLOT_SEED, shl(96, addr))` bakes master seed + address into one word; each loop iteration only needs one mstore + keccak256(0x00, 0x40) to reach the balance slot instead of a nested mapping lookup. (2) Reverse iteration — `for { let i := shl(5, ids.length) } i {}` counts down in 32-byte steps so `i` doubles as a calldata byte offset, eliminating a separate multiply per iteration. (3) Approval check — `keccak256(0x0c, 0x34)` hashes caller + fromSlotSeed in scratch space without touching the free pointer at 0x40. (4) Overflow guard — `if lt(toBalanceAfter, toBalanceBefore)` catches addition overflow without SafeMath. (5) Event emission — calldatacopy writes ids and amounts directly from calldata into memory, skipping ABI re-encoding. (6) Receiver hook — `call` (not staticcall) is used because the hook may be state-changing; returndata is bubble-reverted on failure; magic bytes4 is verified via shl(224, selector) comparison.
when_to_apply: ERC1155 batch transfers where arrays need to be copied to memory for event emission and the receiver hook payload.
when_not_to_apply: When the contract overrides _beforeTokenTransfer or _afterTokenTransferCalldata with non-trivial logic — those hooks wrap this assembly and must be accounted for separately.
